values.value: POSTs a Nebula-Logger-shaped JSON envelope to any HTTP endpoint. Use for integrations that don't fit one of the purpose-built notifiers - internal systems, third-party observability tools, ad-hoc webhooks. Fully customizable HTTP method, path, and headers per recipient.

## Generic Webhook setup
Use this notifier for HTTP endpoints that don't fit one of the purpose-built integrations. The payload is a Nebula-Logger-shaped envelope you can parse on your receiving side.
### Salesforce setup
- **Webhook mode**: paste the endpoint URL into this service's `Webhook URL` field. All recipients hit the same URL (customize path per recipient via config JSON if needed). - **Named Credential mode**: create a Named Credential referencing your endpoint's base URL with whatever auth protocol the endpoint requires (OAuth, Custom header, mutual TLS, etc.). Reference the Named Credential here.
### Recipient config JSON
The recipient's config controls HTTP method, path suffix, and any custom headers on top of the built-in `Content-Type: application/json`:
```json { "method": "POST", "path": "/webhook/nebula", "headers": { "X-Custom-Header": "custom-value" } } ```
- `method` (optional, defaults to POST) - `path` (optional, appended to the Named Credential URL - ignored in webhook mode) - `headers` (optional, arbitrary map of header name to value)
### Payload envelope
Every request has the same top-level shape:
```json { "notificationName": "<rule name>", "sourceSObjectType": "LogEntry__c", "channelIdentifier": "<recipient's ChannelIdentifier__c value if any>", "matchingRecords": [ /* array of the matched source records */ ] } ```
